articles.title: Dow falls 200 pts as Middle East tensions rattle markets, oil prices jump
articles.excerpt: Wall Street's main indexes opened mixed, putting a volatile start to the week on Monday, as rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East offset optimism from a strong earnings backdrop and recent record highs. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 216 points or 0.44%, the S&P

articles.title: Dairy Queen CEO on consumer demand and product innovation
articles.excerpt: Dairy Queen CEO Troy Bader spoke to CNBC's Becky Quick ahead of Berkshire Hathaway's annual shareholder meeting. They discussed the unique consumer environment the company is seeing, how they're expanding internationally, and the impact of GLP-1 usage on the company.

articles.title: See's Candies CEO on consumer demand in a tough economic environment
articles.excerpt: See's Candies President & CEO Pat Egan spoke to CNBC's Becky Quick ahead of Berkshire Hathaway's annual shareholder meeting. They discussed strong sales driven by holidays like Valentine's Day and Easter, managing costs despite rising energy prices, and more.

articles.title: Benjamin Moore CEO on the housing market and consumer demand
articles.excerpt: Benjamin Moore CEO Dan Calkins spoke to CNBC's Becky Quick ahead of Berkshire Hathaway's annual shareholder meeting. They discussed housing market pressures impacting demand, rising raw material costs, the company's focus on cost containment, and more.

articles.title: Tech Giants Prop Up Nasdaq Composite as Oil Shock Tests Market Resilience
articles.excerpt: The Nasdaq Composite (^IXIC) is leaning on its biggest tech names this morning to absorb a fresh oil shock, with index futures hovering near flat after climbing well off their overnight lows.

articles.title: Total Return Forecasts: Major Asset Classes - May 4, 2026
articles.excerpt: The long-term performance outlook for the Global Market Index edged higher in April. Roughly a third of GMI's components are projected to generate softer returns relative to their respective results over the past ten years.

articles.title: We are increasingly seeing a narrower breadth of growth, says EY Parthenon's Greg Daco
articles.excerpt: Gregory Daco, EY-Parthenon chief economist, joins 'Squawk Box' to discuss the state of the economy, impact of the Middle East conflict, impact of AI, the Fed's interest rate outlook, and more.

articles.title: The Economy Is About To Test The Stock Market
articles.excerpt: The S&P 500 has delivered sensational Q1 earnings, with growth rates jumping to 27.1% year-over-year, driven by AI and strong consumer resilience. Despite all-time highs, warning signs include rising inflation, surging oil prices, and deteriorating high-frequency economic data ou

articles.title: Why SPX Momentum May Override Vulnerability
articles.excerpt: “If you are a bull with a six to 12-month horizon, stay the course as the climax in pessimism a few weeks ago continues to unwind via short covering

articles.title: S&P 500 Rises To New Highs As Iran War Moves To Investors' Rear View Mirror
articles.excerpt: The S&P 500 reached new record highs during the trading week ending on Friday, 1 May 2026. Through 1 May 2026, the S&P 500 is just 2.1% below the center of the redzone forecast range, which is a reasonable projection of where the index would be had the geopolitical event not take

articles.title: The market's next test could come down to two stocks
articles.excerpt: AMD and Palantir results will drive the market this week. Options traders have a bullish tilt on the stocks ahead of the reports.

articles.title: Market Correction Risk: Why Summer 2026 Looks Risky
articles.excerpt: The S&P 500 has rallied roughly 14% off its late-March washout to a new high near 7,125. The 14-day relative strength index on the S&P 500 has spent most of the past three weeks above 70, the threshold that has historically marked overbought conditions.

articles.title: The Fed Could Crush Trump's Bull Market — Here's the Warning Wall Street Is Missing
articles.excerpt: Markets have spent months betting that President Donald Trump's second-term agenda would extend the rally that pushed the S&P 500 to repeated highs.

articles.title: Earnings Driving Rally as Stocks Shrug Off Geopolitics
articles.excerpt: Futures are little changed ahead of Monday's open after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq-100 closed at fresh all-time highs last week. Kevin Green breaks down the market disconnect as rising Treasury yields, a stronger U.S. dollar, and higher crude oil prices signal underlying pressure ben

articles.title: Leverage At Extremes And Risk Mispriced. A Strong Sell
articles.excerpt: Markets are not priced for fragility but for continuity. Leverage sits near extremes, while savings rates offer little buffer.

articles.title: Dow futures slip as geopolitical risks overshadow last week's rally
articles.excerpt: 8:15am: Global tensions escalate Stocks are looking a bit shaky out of the gate Monday, as rising geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz keep investors on edge despite last week's strong finish. Dow futures are down about 0.4%, while S&P 500 futures are slipping modestly a

articles.title: EU countries push to settle US trade deal to avoid car tariff hike
articles.excerpt: European Union countries are broadly pushing for the swift implementation of the bloc's side ‌of a trade deal struck with the United States last year to ward off the threat of higher auto tariffs, EU diplomats said on Monday.

articles.title: Stocks still have tailwinds through May into July, says Fundstrat's Tom Lee
articles.excerpt: Tom Lee, Fundstrat CIO and head of research, joins 'Squawk Box' to discuss the latest market trends, impact of the Iran war, state of the AI boom, concerns over software, and more.

articles.title: Food price inflation still rising, as lower income households feel the squeeze, study says
articles.excerpt: UK food prices by November could be 50% higher than they were at the start of the cost-of-living crisis five years ago, analysts have said.

articles.title: European auto stocks fall as Trump threatens new 25% tariff
articles.excerpt: Shares in Volkswagen, BMW and Stellantis fall sharply at the open in Europe after U.S. President Donald Trump says he'll raise tariffs on imported vehicles from 15% to 25% as soon as this week. Trump accused the European Union of breaching last July's trade deal.
